clean_story:
Now I will teach you umbrella.

Take big leaf—stiff like hide, wide like bear’s back—but not alive. Hold stick in middle, long as arm, straight like spear shaft. Leaf stays open, flat above head, blocks rain like rock blocks sun. Wind pushes? Stick keeps leaf steady, no flop like wet fur. When rain stops, fold leaf down small, like bat wings closing.
